task.title: Agent On-Ramp Coach plugin
task.request: Create a cross-agent Agent On-Ramp Coach plugin that helps hesitant engineers adopt AI coding agents through safe, bounded, reviewable workflows.
task.problem_statement: Engineering teams need a reusable plugin that defaults to read-only agent workflows, makes agent work inspectable, gates edits behind explicit confidence levels and approvals, and produces deterministic session artifacts rather than relying on agent self-reporting.

goal: Add a production-usable agent-on-ramp-coach plugin with skill instructions, templates, schema, deterministic Node validation script, examples, hook samples, CI sample, manifests, docs, and tests.
success_criteria: The new plugin lives under plugins/agent-on-ramp-coach with repo-compatible manifests and package metadata.
The agent-on-ramp-coach skill defines read-only defaults, confidence levels, risk rules, approval gates, file and command rules, review summaries, anti-patterns, and cross-agent guidance.
Session templates, examples, and schema represent the requested adoption-session structure and allowed enum values.
The onramp.mjs script uses only Node built-in modules and implements init, check, summary, snapshot, and no-edits.
Valid adoption-session example passes validation and invalid example fails validation.
Snapshot and no-edits checks work in this git repository, including a simulated read-only edit failure.
README, AGENTS fragment, custom-instructions fragment, hook examples, and CI sample are copy-ready and accurate.
Relevant package tests, plugin validation, and marketplace sync checks pass.

scope.summary: Create a new bounded plugin package for safe AI agent adoption workflows.
in_scope: Generated marketplace metadata for the new plugin
out_of_scope: Creating a backend service, MCP server, MCP app, or desktop app
Installing the plugin into a personal marketplace
Changing global or repo instructions outside the requested fragment files

non_goals: Do not make autonomous coding the default.
Do not create vague placeholder docs or examples.
Do not hard-code credentials, local absolute paths, tenant IDs, or production secrets.
Do not modify existing plugin packages except generated marketplace output if required by repo conventions.
expected_behavior: The plugin provides a safe Agent On-Ramp workflow with read-only defaults, confidence levels, risk classification, explicit edit approvals, deterministic adoption-session checks, git snapshot/no-edit enforcement, copy-ready docs, and validation examples.
current_behavior: No agent-on-ramp-coach plugin exists in this repository.
test_requirements: Validate the plugin manifest with the plugin-creator validator.
Run the valid adoption-session example through onramp.mjs check.
Run the invalid adoption-session example and confirm check fails.
Run snapshot and no-edits in a git repository and confirm no-edits passes with no out-of-session changes.
Simulate a file change outside .agent/session for a read-only level and confirm no-edits fails.
Run npm test from the new plugin package.
rollback_plan: Remove plugins/agent-on-ramp-coach, restore generated plugins/marketplace.json if changed, and restore local .agent/session gate artifacts if needed.
stop_conditions: The requested behavior requires external dependencies.
The plugin cannot satisfy repo manifest or plugin validation contracts without changing existing infrastructure.
No-edit enforcement cannot be made deterministic using git state.
Validation requires modifying existing plugins or repository instructions outside the allowed scope.
A generated example or schema fails validation and cannot be fixed within scope.
implementation_plan: Scaffold the plugin directory using the plugin-creator flow where applicable.
Add repo-compatible manifests, package metadata, changelog, license, and orchestrator agent.
Implement the skill, templates, schema, examples, hook samples, CI sample, and documentation fragments.
Implement onramp.mjs with built-in Node modules and deterministic validation/no-edit checks.
Add node:test coverage for valid, invalid, init, snapshot, no-edits pass, and no-edits fail behavior.
Run plugin validation, package tests, targeted script checks, snapshot/no-edits smoke checks, and marketplace sync.
final_acceptance_checklist: The plugin content is copy-ready and avoids hype, placeholders, and autonomous defaults.
The skill body stays under the repository line-count guidance.
The script has clear errors and no external dependencies.
Read-only sessions fail when files outside .agent/session change after a snapshot.
Docs explain hooks as enforcement aids, not universal skill-selection mechanisms.
All required validation commands have been run or any unrun command is clearly reported.
